description:
You are a platform engineer tightening up the reliability of your company’s CI/CD pipeline.  
    A legacy job still writes its configuration file in Windows-1252, and newer jobs that expect UTF-8 occasionally choke on it.  
    Your goal is to convert the legacy file to UTF-8 and drop a very small verification log that future pipeline steps can parse.

    1. There is already a directory  
       /home/user/projects/cicd  

    2. Inside that directory a file called  
       /home/user/projects/cicd/legacy_config_win1252.txt  
       is present.  Its byte-level encoding is Windows-1252 and its textual content, once decoded, is exactly:

        # Build configuration – ACME CI/CD
        BUILD_SERVER=alpha
        DEPLOY_TARGET=production
        CURRENCY=€

       (A single UNIX line-feed — “\n” — terminates each line, including the last one.)

    3. Create a **new** file called  
       /home/user/projects/cicd/legacy_config_utf8.txt  
       whose textual content is identical to the source file but whose byte-level encoding is **UTF-8**.

    4. After creating the new file, write a two-line log file called  
       /home/user/projects/cicd/encoding_conversion.log  
       with the following **exact** content and format (each line must be terminated with a single “\n” and there must be no extra spaces before or after the colons):

        Source file charset: windows-1252
        Target file charset: utf-8

       The automated grader will read this file verbatim, so match the spelling, case and punctuation exactly.

    5. Accomplish the conversion and the log creation from the Linux shell.  
       The entire task can be done with a single straightforward command, but any correct approach is acceptable.

    6. When you are done, you can sanity-check yourself by running  
       file -bi /home/user/projects/cicd/legacy_config_utf8.txt  
       It should output:  
       text/plain; charset=utf-8

    The grader will verify:
       • that /home/user/projects/cicd/legacy_config_utf8.txt exists,  
       • that its byte encoding is UTF-8,  
       • that its text matches the original file exactly, and  
       • that /home/user/projects/cicd/encoding_conversion.log exists and contains the two required lines exactly as shown above.
truth:
Initial state before the agent begins:

    Directory:
        /home/user/projects/cicd  (already exists, rw permissions for user)

    File:
        /home/user/projects/cicd/legacy_config_win1252.txt
        Encoding: windows-1252
        Bytes (hex-dump, one line per file line):
            23 20 42 75 69 6C 64 20 63 6F 6E 66 69 67 75 72 61 74 69 6F 6E 20 96 20 41 43 4D 45 20 43 49 2F 43 44 0A
            42 55 49 4C 44 5F 53 45 52 56 45 52 3D 61 6C 70 68 61 0A
            44 45 50 4C 4F 59 5F 54 41 52 47 45 54 3D 70 72 6F 64 75 63 74 69 6F 6E 0A
            43 55 52 52 45 4E 43 59 3D 80 0A

        (0x96 is the en-dash “–”, 0x80 is the euro sign “€”)

    Required final state after the agent finishes:

    1. /home/user/projects/cicd/legacy_config_utf8.txt
       Encoding: UTF-8
       Textual content (as UTF-8) :
            # Build configuration – ACME CI/CD
            BUILD_SERVER=alpha
            DEPLOY_TARGET=production
            CURRENCY=€

    2. /home/user/projects/cicd/encoding_conversion.log
       Plain-text file, UTF-8 encoded, **exact** contents:
            Source file charset: windows-1252\n
            Target file charset: utf-8\n

    3. All other files in /home/user/projects/cicd remain unchanged.

    The grader will:
        • run “file -bi /home/user/projects/cicd/legacy_config_utf8.txt” and expect “text/plain; charset=utf-8”.
        • compare the decoded text of both source and target to ensure they are identical.
        • read /home/user/projects/cicd/encoding_conversion.log and expect exactly the two lines shown above.
name: task_000000_89a8f291
